user_query:
Please create a full, fresh Mandarin daily practice session for me and write the outputs to files. Read my preferences and guardrails from input/preferences.json and input/avoid_list.json. Then: 1) Generate the complete session in output/mandarin_drill_session.md using exactly these section headers on their own lines in this order: "1) Vocabulary", "2) Grammar pattern", "3) Character focus", "4) Tone drill", "5) Reading passage", "6) Speaking prompt", "7) Quick quiz", "Cultural note". 2) Also create a summary file at output/mandarin_drill_meta.json with fields that capture hsk_level, topic, vocab_count, characters_count, minimal_pairs_count, quiz_count, and a boolean no_banned_content indicating whether you honored the banned lists.

Formatting and constraints for output/mandarin_drill_session.md:
- 1) Vocabulary: Provide exactly 10 items numbered 1–10. For each, include six lines with these exact labels in this order: "- Characters:", "- Pinyin:", "- Tone:", "- Meaning:", "- Example:", "- Memory tip:". Include pinyin with tone marks, and in the Tone line include tone number notation (e.g., 3rd, 4th). Do not include any vocabulary whose English Meaning matches any banned term from input/avoid_list.json.
- 2) Grammar pattern: Include the following labeled lines or blocks in this order: "Pattern name (English):", "Structure:", "Explanation:", "Examples:" (with 3 examples, each including Chinese, pinyin with tone marks, and English), "Common mistakes:", "Comparison:". The pattern’s English name must not match any banned pattern from input/avoid_list.json.
- 3) Character focus: Because my level is HSK 3+, include exactly 3 characters, numbered 1–3, each with these labeled lines in this order: "Character:", "Pinyin:", "Radical:", "Strokes:", "Compounds:", "Memory tip:".
- 4) Tone drill: Provide exactly 5 minimal pairs, numbered 1–5. Format each as: "WordA (pinyin with tone marks) vs WordB (pinyin with tone marks) — meanings". Favor pairs that help with 3rd tone if my preferences request it.
- 5) Reading passage: Write a level-appropriate short passage for my level, in 5–8 sentences. For each sentence, first give the Chinese line, and immediately below it a line that begins with "Pinyin:" followed by the pinyin with tone marks. After all sentences, include a block titled "English translation:" with the full translation. End this section with "Highlighted points:" followed by 3 bullet points referencing vocabulary or grammar from the passage.
- 6) Speaking prompt: Include "Scenario:", then "Sample dialogue:" with at least 4 turns labeled clearly, then "Practice prompts:" (3 prompts), and "Suggested vocabulary:" (at least 6 items with tone marks).
- 7) Quick quiz: Provide exactly 5 questions (mixed types) numbered 1–5. After the questions, include a line with exactly four hyphens "----" and then an "Answers:" block listing answers 1–5.
- Cultural note: End with 2–3 sentences on a relevant cultural topic.

Additional requirements:
- Personalize to the HSK level and topic preferences from input/preferences.json. Keep difficulty appropriate to that level.
- Ensure pinyin uses proper tone marks throughout, and include tone number notation in the Vocabulary section.
- Avoid any English-meaning vocabulary terms listed in the banned vocabulary of input/avoid_list.json, and avoid any banned grammar pattern English names for the Grammar pattern section.
- In output/mandarin_drill_meta.json, set hsk_level to match the value in input/preferences.json, set topic to the primary topic requested, set vocab_count=10, characters_count=3 (HSK 3+), minimal_pairs_count=5, quiz_count=5, and set no_banned_content=true if you complied with the banned lists.

Write only the two files under output/ as specified.
